diffs.neu: {catName} has no home. What kind of cat is he?
diffs.sent: I am {catName}. I am a stray cat.

diffs.neu: Where does {catName} go every night?
diffs.sent: Every night I visit one yard.

diffs.neu: Type what {catName} does to get on the wall.
diffs.sent: I jump on the low wall.

diffs.neu: Type what {dogName}'s tail does when {catName} arrives.
diffs.sent: {dogName} wags his tail.

diffs.neu: Type the word for what {catName} and {dogName} are to each other.
diffs.sent: We are friends.

diffs.neu: Type the word that describes the night in the story.
diffs.sent: It is a dark night.

diffs.neu: What part of the kitten's body is soaked by the rain?
diffs.sent: Her fur is all wet.

diffs.neu: Type the number of days the kitten has not eaten.
diffs.sent: She has not eaten in two days.

diffs.neu: What does the kitten do because she is so cold?
diffs.sent: She shakes from cold.

diffs.neu: What does the kitten do with her body to keep warm?
diffs.sent: She curls into a small ball.

diffs.neu: Type the word that says she has no one with her.
diffs.sent: She is alone.

diffs.neu: Where is the kitten hiding in the rain?
diffs.sent: The alley is wet and quiet.

diffs.neu: What does the kitten see coming closer in the dark?
diffs.sent: A big shadow comes closer.

diffs.neu: Type the word for who the big shadow really is.
diffs.sent: It is a woman.

diffs.neu: Type the verb that says the rain no longer hits the cat.
diffs.sent: The rain stops on the cat.

diffs.neu: Type the verb for what the cat does with her ears.
diffs.sent: The cat listens to her.

diffs.neu: Type the adjective that describes Grandma's voice.
diffs.sent: Her voice is gentle.

diffs.neu: Type the adjective for a cat who says nothing.
diffs.sent: She is silent.

diffs.old: Type the adjective you hear first.
diffs.neu: Type the word that tells you the towel is not dirty.
diffs.sent: The towel is clean and dry.

diffs.neu: Type the word for how the cat starts to feel after drying off.
diffs.sent: The cat begins to feel warm.

diffs.neu: Type the word for what Grandma suddenly has in her head.
diffs.sent: But she has an idea.

diffs.neu: Type the word that says the cat may come whenever she likes.
diffs.sent: You are welcome any time.

diffs.neu: Grandma already told one story. What does she want to tell?
diffs.sent: Grandma says, "I have another story for you."

diffs.neu: In what season does the ant and grasshopper story happen?
diffs.sent: It is a short story about summer.

diffs.neu: Type where the ant and grasshopper live.
diffs.sent: In the green grass live an ant and a grasshopper.

diffs.neu: Type what the ant carries back home.
diffs.sent: The ant carries food to her home.

diffs.neu: Type how the grasshopper feels in winter with no food.
diffs.sent: He is cold and hungry.

diffs.neu: Type the word for how the wind and the sun describe themselves.
diffs.sent: Both said, "I am stronger than you."

diffs.neu: Type how hard the traveler holds his coat in the wind.
diffs.sent: The traveler holds his coat tight.

diffs.neu: Type the verb for what the traveler does with his coat in the sun.
diffs.sent: The traveler takes off his coat.

diffs.neu: Type the word that means the hour is no longer early.
diffs.sent: It is late.

diffs.neu: Type the direction {catName} walks at the end of the night.
diffs.sent: I walk back slowly.

diffs.neu: When does {catName} jump on the wall?
diffs.sent: I jump on the wall again tonight.

diffs.neu: {dogName} is excited. What does her tail do on the floor?
diffs.sent: {dogName} thumps her tail on the floor.

diffs.neu: What does the giant peach do on the water?
diffs.sent: The peach floated toward her hands.

diffs.neu: What does the old woman do with the peach?
diffs.sent: She lifted the peach out of the water.

diffs.neu: How does Momotaro's team cross the ocean?
diffs.sent: They got into a small boat and sailed.

diffs.neu: What season is it on this autumn night in the yard?
diffs.sent: The autumn wind is cool tonight.

diffs.neu: How does {catName} get up onto the low wall?
diffs.sent: I climb the low wall again.

diffs.neu: Type what {dogName} does with his eyes when {catName} arrives.
diffs.sent: He looks up at me.

diffs.neu: Type how the duckling feels inside the egg.
diffs.sent: It is dark and warm here.

diffs.neu: Type what the duckling can do well in the pond.
diffs.sent: I can swim very well.

diffs.neu: Type the word the other ducklings call him.
diffs.sent: You are so ugly.

diffs.neu: Type how the duckling feels walking through the swamp.
diffs.sent: I feel alone.

diffs.neu: Type what the duckling does when he hears the hunters.
diffs.sent: I hide in the tall grass.

diffs.neu: Type the word for how the duckling's feet feel on the frozen lake.
diffs.sent: My feet are stuck.

diffs.neu: Type what the farmer does for the half-frozen duckling.
diffs.sent: He saves my life.

diffs.neu: Type what {catName} does about himself after the story.
diffs.sent: I think about myself.

diffs.neu: Type what {catName} does to get onto the wall tonight.
diffs.sent: Tonight I climb the wall again.

diffs.neu: Type what Grandma does as {catName} arrives.
diffs.sent: Grandma waits for me.

diffs.neu: What does {dogName}'s tail do on the grass tonight?
diffs.sent: {dogName} thumps his tail on the grass.

diffs.neu: Type what Grandma is holding tonight.
diffs.sent: She holds a story book.

diffs.neu: What does the Hare do about his own speed?
diffs.sent: He brags about his speed.

diffs.neu: What does the Tortoise say to the Hare's challenge?
diffs.sent: Tortoise nods. "I accept."

diffs.neu: Type how the Hare runs at the start of the race.
diffs.sent: He runs as fast as he can.

diffs.neu: Type the word the Hare thinks he has plenty of.
diffs.sent: I have plenty of time.

diffs.neu: Type the word for what the Hare quickly becomes under the bush.
diffs.sent: Hare falls asleep fast.

diffs.neu: Type the place the Tortoise walks past.
diffs.sent: The river is wide and blue.

diffs.neu: Type how the Tortoise feels but keeps going.
diffs.sent: I am tired but I am okay.

diffs.neu: Type the word for the end of the race.
diffs.sent: I see the finish line.

diffs.neu: Type the verb the Tortoise says when he reaches the tree.
diffs.sent: I made it to the end.

diffs.neu: Type what the Hare does when he finally wakes up.
diffs.sent: Hare runs as fast as he can.

diffs.neu: Type what the Crow is holding in his beak.
diffs.sent: The cheese smells very good.

diffs.neu: How does the Fox trick the Crow with sweet words?
diffs.sent: He flatters the crow with sweet words.

diffs.neu: Type what the Crow does with his beak to sing.
diffs.sent: The crow opens his beak to sing.

diffs.neu: Type the word that describes the country mouse's food.
diffs.sent: The food is simple but warm.

diffs.neu: Type what {dogName} does as the story ends.
diffs.sent: {dogName} sighs and closes his eyes.

diffs.neu: Type the place {catName} walks back to at the end.
diffs.sent: The alley is dark but safe tonight.

diffs.neu: What do slow steps still do for you?
diffs.sent: Slow steps still carry you forward.

diffs.neu: Type the word Grandma calls the tortoise's small magic.
diffs.sent: Patience is a small magic.

diffs.neu: Type what {catName} does up onto the wall.
diffs.sent: I jump up on the wall.

diffs.neu: Type what Grandma turns slowly in the book.
diffs.sent: She turns the page slowly.

diffs.neu: Type what the Dog wears around his neck.
diffs.sent: The Dog had his collar.

diffs.neu: Type the word that says the Camel will not work.
diffs.sent: He is very lazy.

diffs.neu: Type the verb the Dog asks the Camel to do.
diffs.sent: The Dog asks him to carry too.

diffs.neu: Type which animal the three complain about to Man.
diffs.sent: The Camel will not work for us.

diffs.neu: Type what the Camel stares at on the ground.
diffs.sent: He stares at a pool of water.

diffs.neu: Type the adjective the Djinn uses for the Camel's neck.
diffs.sent: My long and bubbling friend.

diffs.neu: Type what the Camel may not do for three days.
diffs.sent: You will work three days without eating.

diffs.neu: Type where {catName} walks back to at the end of the night.
diffs.sent: I walk back to my corner.

diffs.neu: How does tonight feel to {catName} compared to other nights?
diffs.sent: Tonight feels colder than other nights.

diffs.neu: Type the verb {catName} does to get up on the wall.
diffs.sent: I jump on the low wall.

diffs.old: Type the body part you hear.
diffs.neu: Type the body part of {dogName} that bends back when he is scared.
diffs.sent: His ears bend back.

diffs.neu: Type how Grandma's dark old book feels to {catName}.
diffs.sent: It is a little scary.

diffs.neu: Type the adverb that says Vasilisa works a lot every day.
diffs.sent: She works hard every day.

diffs.neu: Type what Vasilisa does with the little doll in her pocket.
diffs.sent: She holds the doll tight.

diffs.old: Type the time word you hear.
diffs.neu: Type the time word that says she walks a very long time.
diffs.sent: She walks for many hours.

diffs.neu: Type what the chicken-leg hut can do on its legs.
diffs.sent: The hut can turn around.

diffs.neu: Type the adjective that describes Baba Yaga's teeth.
diffs.sent: Her teeth are old and sharp.

diffs.old: Type the body part you hear.
diffs.neu: Type the body part the doll opens to eat the bread.
diffs.sent: The doll opens her tiny mouth.

diffs.neu: Type the verb for what the doll does while Vasilisa sleeps.
diffs.sent: The doll works while she sleeps.

diffs.neu: Type how the yard looks after the doll's secret work.
diffs.sent: The yard is clean and bright.

diffs.neu: Type the verb for what the skull does to every tree in the forest.
diffs.sent: The skull lights every tree.

diffs.neu: Type the verb that says the fisherman got the magic fish.
diffs.sent: One day he caught a magic fish.

diffs.neu: Type the verb for what the child does to each of the seven beds.
diffs.sent: The child tried each bed slowly.

diffs.neu: Type the verb for what the child does to escape.
diffs.sent: The child woke and ran away fast.

diffs.neu: Type how Grandma smiles at {catName} and {dogName}.
diffs.sent: Grandma smiles softly at us.

diffs.old: Type the body part you hear.
diffs.neu: Type the body part of the chicken-leg hut.
diffs.sent: Why does the hut have chicken legs?

diffs.neu: Type how the night now feels after the scary story is done.
diffs.sent: The night feels softer now.

diffs.neu: How is the yard tonight when {catName} arrives?
diffs.sent: Tonight the yard is quiet.

diffs.neu: Type what {catName} does onto the low wall.
diffs.sent: I jump on the low wall again.

diffs.neu: Type the word that says Grandma is already on her chair.
diffs.sent: Grandma is here.

diffs.neu: What does {dogName} do near Grandma's feet?
diffs.sent: {dogName} lies down by her feet.

diffs.neu: What does Grandma call tonight's story?
diffs.sent: Tonight is a quiet tale.

diffs.neu: Type the number of swans in tonight's story.
diffs.sent: It is about six swans.

diffs.old: Type the time word you hear.
diffs.neu: Type the time word for when the king's children were still happy.
diffs.sent: The children were happy then.

diffs.neu: What do the six swans do into the sky?
diffs.sent: Six swans rose into the sky.

diffs.neu: Type the verb for how the sister leaves toward the wood.
diffs.sent: She walked toward the wood.

diffs.neu: Type what the sister does for her brothers at dusk.
diffs.sent: She waited for the swans.

diffs.neu: Type the verb for how she answers the deal silently.
diffs.sent: She nodded and said yes.

diffs.neu: Type the noun for what she made and is keeping.
diffs.sent: She kept her promise.

diffs.neu: Type the verb for what she does with the six shirts.
diffs.sent: She hid the shirts in a chest.

diffs.neu: Type the noun that rises fast from the fire.
diffs.sent: The smoke rose fast.

diffs.neu: Type the verb for what the woodcutter and his wife do about every wish.
diffs.sent: They argued about every wish.

diffs.neu: Type what the sun says is stronger than him.
diffs.sent: The sun said a cloud was stronger.

diffs.neu: Type the word for how the night feels at the end of the tale.
diffs.sent: The night is still.

diffs.neu: Type the adjective that says silence can also be a kind of courage.
diffs.sent: Silence can be brave too.

diffs.neu: Type the verb Ye Xian must do with water from the well.
diffs.sent: Ye Xian must carry water from the well.

diffs.neu: Type the adjective that describes Ye Xian's voice by the pond.
diffs.sent: Her voice is very gentle by the water.

diffs.neu: Type what the stepmother does next to the pond, pretending to be Ye Xian.
diffs.sent: She walks slowly to the pond and leans down.

diffs.neu: Type the verb for what Ye Xian does in the empty water.
diffs.sent: She searches the water but finds nothing.

diffs.neu: Type the verb the old man tells her to do with the fish bones.
diffs.sent: He says, bury the bones under the cave.

diffs.neu: Type what Ye Xian whispers to the bones.
diffs.sent: She whispers a small wish to the bones.

diffs.neu: Type the verb for how Ye Xian asks the bones for help.
diffs.sent: She runs to the cave and begs the bones.

diffs.neu: Type the verb for what Ye Xian does under the lanterns.
diffs.sent: She dances slowly under the bright lanterns.

diffs.neu: Type the verb for how Ye Xian leaves the festival.
diffs.sent: She runs as fast as her small feet allow.

diffs.neu: Type the adjective that says the golden slipper does not fit other women.
diffs.sent: Many women try the slipper but it is too small.

diffs.neu: Type the part of the snail the kind girl steps out of.
diffs.sent: A kind girl steps out of the shell.

diffs.neu: Type the noun for what the gods give Hou Yi.
diffs.sent: The gods give him a small bottle of medicine.

diffs.old: Type the time word you hear.
diffs.neu: Type the time word for when {catName} is not sure he will be back.
diffs.sent: I do not know if I will come back tomorrow.

diffs.neu: Type the word that means scared but still doing it.
diffs.sent: Being brave is not the same as not being scared.
